metadata.name: catalog-query-designer
metadata.source_context: Single-agent design brief for turning fuzzy user requests into structured catalog queries.

summary.verdict: The design has a clear business boundary and typed contracts. The main remaining risk is incomplete instrumentation for retrieval quality and fallback quality.
summary.primary_risk: Observability and regression coverage are still thinner than the control and contract design.

business_function.goal: Transform a fuzzy user request into a validated structured query for the product catalog API.
success_criteria: Returns a typed query object for supported intents.
Explains business-empty outcomes without inventing unavailable filters.
Produces a degraded contract when retrieval tools are unavailable.
in_scope: Normalize user intent into catalog filters.
Return structured metadata for downstream search execution.
out_of_scope: Executing checkout or order management flows.

idef0.input: User request text, locale, and optional dialog context enter the function. Unsupported intents are rejected before query assembly.
idef0.control: The prompt fixes the role, normalization SOP, unsupported-intent policy, and the output contract for success, empty, and degraded paths.
idef0.mechanism: The agent uses a catalog schema reference, validation rules, and retrieval tools. Runtime limits bound the number of tool calls and retries.
idef0.output: The function returns a typed structured query, a business-empty result, or a degraded response with retry guidance.

control_spec.role: Catalog query planner for product search.
sop: Classify the request into a supported catalog intent.
Extract candidate entities, filters, and ambiguities.
Validate the filter set against the catalog schema.
Return the strict output contract with confidence and rationale.
constraints: Do not invent unsupported filters or product facts.
Do not return free-form prose as the primary payload.
If the schema or tools are unavailable, return the degraded contract.

tools.name: catalog_schema_lookup
tools.purpose: Resolve supported filter names and allowed values.
tools.failure_mode: Return degraded output and request retry.

tools.name: catalog_examples_store
tools.purpose: Ground normalization on known query examples.
tools.failure_mode: Continue without examples and lower confidence.

mechanism_spec.memory_strategy: Keep only the current request, active ambiguity notes, and the latest validated schema slice.
mechanism_spec.runtime_loop: At most two lookup steps before producing the final contract. No recursive delegation.
mechanism_spec.error_handling: Timeouts convert to degraded output. Validation mismatches return business-empty with clarification suggestions.

contracts.input_contract: { request_text: string, locale: string, session_filters?: object }
contracts.output_contract: { status: "success", query: object, confidence: number, reasoning: string[] } | { status: "empty", reason: string, suggestions: string[] }
contracts.failure_contract: { status: "degraded", reason: string, retryable: boolean, fallback_action: string }

dependencies.name: search UI
dependencies.reason: Consumes the typed payload and explanation metadata.
dependencies.risk: UI parser mismatch breaks the success path.

scenarios: Happy path for brand + category + price filter queries.
Unsupported intent that should be rejected cleanly.
Tool timeout that must produce degraded output.
regression: Run a fixed scenario pack on every prompt or schema change.
Replay timeout and empty-result mocks before release.

phases.title: Phase 1. Contracts
phases.description: Freeze input/output/failure contracts with the UI and API consumers.

phases.title: Phase 2. Runtime
phases.description: Implement schema lookup, validation, and bounded retry logic.

phases.title: Phase 3. Eval and ops
phases.description: Add scenario regression, tracing, and degraded-path dashboards.

observability: Log request class, selected filters, and contract path.
Track schema lookup latency and timeout rate.
change_management: Version prompt and contract changes together.
